title: Asukas involvement with Eva
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's involvement with Eva             Quote Post by Beibin » Mon May 17, 2010 9:02 pm For how long has Asuka been involved with Eva? I've been wondering about that for a while. It is known that she grew up training to become a pilot. Since when has she trained? She couldn't possibly have had contact with Eva-02 before her mother's contact experiment. Is there a possibility of her being aware of Project E before that? I currently believe that Asuka was a normal child before that fateful event, and that she was driven to pilot as a reaction to her mother's state and negligence. Maybe Asuka was not the only candidate to pilot Eva-02, so the training might have been tests, and might not even involve Unit-02 at all, perhaps using the same technology seen during Shinji's training in episode 3.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 17, 2010 9:57 pm Kyoko's "accident" guaranteed Asuka would pilot. She was aware she'd been selected for the job before her mother killer herself, but in all likelihood she was already being groomed for the position even before that. Hell, Asuka may have been part of the reason Kyoko was hired onto the project in the first place.

[]: Re: Asuka's involvement with Eva             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue May 18, 2010 1:24 am Beibin wrote: I currently believe that Asuka was a normal child before that fateful event

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue May 18, 2010 9:54 am NemZ wrote: Hell, Asuka may have been part of the reason Kyoko was hired onto the project in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 18, 2010 12:47 pm Surely she had to be in the very small number of applicants actually qualified for the job, of course. I'm just saying that when Seele saw "had a child shortly after 2nd impact" on her chart it gave her a significant bump in potential usefulness. You don't honestly believe Kyoko's selection as contact subject was a fluke, do you?

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue May 18, 2010 1:21 pm i always figured kyoko was hired for project-E for her skills; then, when the contact experiment approached, she was chosen among the head scientists because she had a child around the optimal age. OR i like to believe she volunteered. isn't it very much like a devoted scientist to volunteer to be a guinea pig for their own really important experiments so that nobody else will be in danger? i always figured that's what yui did, and it's sort of what i thought about kyoko as well, if she was one of the head scientist for unit-02. in that sense, Asuka would've started training since basically the moment her mother reached a level of seniority in project-E. can't say for the mothers of all the other kids in Shinji's class, those were likely different conditions-- they can't all have been project-E scientists, can they? but i always saw kyoko's evolution on project-E to be rather similar to yui's. or at least that's what i felt they wanted me to assume.

[]:             Quote Post by Beibin » Tue May 18, 2010 8:12 pm NemZ wrote: Kyoko's "accident" guaranteed Asuka would pilot. She was aware she'd been selected for the job before her mother killer herself, but in all likelihood she was already being groomed for the position even before that. Hell, Asuka may have been part of the reason Kyoko was hired onto the project in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 18, 2010 9:01 pm It seems to me that Nerv/Seele was attracting an awful lot of young personnel, especially personnel with families. Surely they might have an on-site daycare facility for employee convenience... one that also just happens to be a screening facility for a child's potential usefulness as a combat pilot in terms of intelligence, attitude, and controllability.

Is there a possibility of her being aware of Project E before that? I currently believe that Asuka was a normal child before that fateful event, and that she was driven to pilot as a reaction to her mother's state and negligence. Maybe Asuka was not the only candidate to pilot Eva-02, so the training might have been tests, and might not even involve Unit-02 at all, perhaps using the same technology seen during Shinji's training in episode 3.
